title: The winter (?) of our discontent
summary:
Main
The three things investors should know this week
Surface risks are abating. Rates are on their way down, inflation remains cool, the equity market is still at an all-time high with a broad rally and the bond market is pricing in more realistic year-end rates. At the same time, underlying risks, mostly linked to high debt and an exacerbating political climate, are rising.

China's Real Economic Crisis
Why Beijing Won't Give Up on a Failing Model
By Zongyuan Zoe Liu | August 6, 2024
The Chinese economy is stuck. Following Beijing's decision, in late 2022, to abruptly end its draconian "zero COVID" policy, many observers assumed that China's growth engine would rapidly reignite.
